# Immutable Infrastructure Documentation

> Immutable Infrastructure product documentation covering Cluster API-based Kubernetes cluster lifecycle management, provider support, installation, upgrades, node operations, machine configuration, and Kubernetes API references.

Documentation for Immutable Infrastructure, built with Doom and MDX.
Includes user guides, provider-specific workflows, operational how-to content, and generated Kubernetes API reference pages.


## Root

- [docs/en/index.mdx](docs/en/index.mdx): Top-level navigation page for the English immutable infrastructure docs, built around a generated Overview component with no custom overview headers. It functions as the entry point that exposes child documentation pages rather than carrying detailed procedural content itself.

## apis

- [docs/en/apis/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/index.mdx): API Reference landing page ordered late in the navigation through frontmatter weight 90. The page renders an Overview component to list the API reference children under the `API Reference` heading.
- [docs/en/apis/intro.mdx](docs/en/apis/intro.mdx): Introduction page for the API Reference section, ordered early with weight 10. Its content is delegated to the `AcpApisOverview` component, making it the starting point for a generated ACP API overview.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/bare-metal/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/bare-metal/index.mdx): Bare Metal Provider APIs overview page that marks the bare-metal API documentation as planned rather than available. It names the intended resources `MetalCluster`, `MetalMachine`, and `MetalMachineTemplate` for bare-metal Kubernetes cluster infrastructure, servers, and server configuration templates.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcscluster.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcscluster.mdx): HCSCluster API reference page for the `hcsclusters.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. Search metadata targets HCS cluster CRD and spec queries, and the page embeds `K8sCrd` to render the Huawei Cloud Stack infrastructure cluster resource details.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachine.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachine.mdx): HCSMachine API reference page for the `hcsmachines.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. The frontmatter targets HCS machine spec and virtual machine searches, while the body uses `K8sCrd` to present the resource schema.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachineconfigpool.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachineconfigpool.mdx): HCSMachineConfigPool API reference page for the `hcsmachineconfigpools.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. Its metadata emphasizes HCS config pool and machine config pool lookup terms, and the `K8sCrd` component supplies the CRD reference content.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachinetemplate.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/hcsmachinetemplate.mdx): HCSMachineTemplate API reference page for the `hcsmachinetemplates.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. It is indexed for HCS machine template and VM template queries and renders the CRD through the `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-cloud-stack/index.mdx): Huawei Cloud Stack Provider APIs overview for infrastructure custom resources used to manage HCS-backed Kubernetes clusters. It lists `HCSCluster`, `HCSMachine`, `HCSMachineTemplate`, and `HCSMachineConfigPool`, links to each reference page, and states that all HCS resources use API group `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcscluster.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcscluster.mdx): DCSCluster API reference page for the `dcsclusters.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. The page consists of a weighted reference entry that renders the resource details through the `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsiphostnamepool.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsiphostnamepool.mdx): DCSIpHostnamePool API reference page for the `dcsiphostnamepools.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. It provides the reference hook for the DCS IP and hostname pool resource through the embedded `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsmachine.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsmachine.mdx): DCSMachine API reference page for the `dcsmachines.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. The page uses `K8sCrd` to generate the DCS machine resource reference content.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsmachinetemplate.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/dcsmachinetemplate.mdx): DCSMachineTemplate API reference page for the `dcsmachinetemplates.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. It exposes the DCS machine template resource schema by embedding the `K8sCrd` renderer.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/huawei-dcs/index.mdx): Huawei DCS Provider APIs overview for custom resources that manage DCS infrastructure. It lists `DCSCluster`, `DCSMachine`, `DCSMachineTemplate`, and `DCSIpHostnamePool`, links to their CRD reference pages, and identifies `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1` as the shared API group.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/index.mdx): Provider APIs section landing page ordered with weight 10. It contains a generated Overview component under the `Provider APIs` heading to surface the provider-specific API reference pages.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/index.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/index.mdx): VMware vSphere Provider APIs overview for Cluster API infrastructure resources used to manage vSphere-backed Kubernetes clusters. It lists the supported CRDs, including `VSphereCluster`, `VSphereClusterIdentity`, `VSphereMachine`, `VSphereVM`, templates, machine config pools, failure domains, and deployment zones, and notes that documented resources use `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1` while `VSphereClusterTemplate` is intentionally out of scope.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherecluster.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherecluster.mdx): VSphereCluster API reference page for the `vsphereclusters.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in `infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1`. The page is indexed for vSphere cluster infrastructure and spec lookups and renders the resource schema through the `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vsphereclusteridentity.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vsphereclusteridentity.mdx): VSphereClusterIdentity API reference page for the cluster-scoped vCenter credential resource used by multiple `VSphereCluster` objects across namespaces, with namespace selectors controlling access. It contrasts this shared-credential model with the guide workflow that references per-cluster Secrets through `VSphereCluster.spec.identityRef`, then embeds the `vsphereclusteridentities.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheredeploymentzone.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheredeploymentzone.mdx): VSphereDeploymentZone API reference page for the `vspheredeploymentzones.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD. The page targets deployment-zone and placement-constraint searches and renders the vSphere placement resource schema through the `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherefailuredomain.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherefailuredomain.mdx): VSphereFailureDomain API reference page for the `vspherefailuredomains.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD. It is indexed for vSphere failure-domain and datacenter-zone queries and uses `K8sCrd` to expose the schema for datacenter, compute, datastore, and network topology resources.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachine.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachine.mdx): VSphereMachine API reference page for the `vspheremachines.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD in the vSphere infrastructure provider. The page is searchable by machine spec and virtual-machine terms and embeds the CRD schema with `K8sCrd`.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachineconfigpool.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachineconfigpool.mdx): VSphereMachineConfigPool API reference page for the `vspheremachineconfigpools.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD. It provides the reference entry for vSphere machine configuration pools, which the surrounding guides use to predefine node slots, hostnames, static IP assignments, NIC settings, and persistent disks.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachinetemplate.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspheremachinetemplate.mdx): VSphereMachineTemplate API reference page for the `vspheremachinetemplates.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD. The page is indexed for vSphere machine template and VM template searches and renders the template schema through the `K8sCrd` component.
- [docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherevm.mdx](docs/en/apis/providers/vmware-vsphere/vspherevm.mdx): VSphereVM API reference page for the low-level provider resource that represents the actual vCenter virtual machine backing a `VSphereMachine`. It explains that controllers create the resource, while operators inspect it for cloning, IP allocation, address reporting, or power-state troubleshooting, and embeds the `vspherevms.infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io` CRD.

## create-cluster

- [docs/en/create-cluster/bare-metal.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/bare-metal.mdx): Bare-metal cluster creation page marking the provider as planned rather than currently available. It names expected future capabilities such as BMC/IPMI remote management, in-place upgrades, hardware inventory, and bare-metal network configuration, and links readers to the bare metal provider overview.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): End-to-end YAML guide for creating Huawei Cloud Stack Kubernetes clusters with Cluster API in the `cpaas-system` namespace. It covers plugin prerequisites, HCS credential Secrets, `HCSMachineConfigPool`, `HCSMachineTemplate`, `KubeadmControlPlane`, `HCSCluster`, ELB and Hybrid Load Balancing requirements, subnet and static IP planning, kubeadm security settings, verification commands, and links to worker-node management and upgrade flows.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/huawei-dcs.mdx): Huawei DCS cluster creation guide covering both the Fleet Essentials web UI workflow and YAML manifests for Cluster API. It details prerequisites such as cloud credentials, IP pools, DCS machine templates, MicroOS VM templates, network connectivity, API load balancers, public registry configuration, then walks through UI wizard fields, `KubeadmControlPlane`, `DCSCluster`, `Cluster`, verification, and a full appendix kubeadm configuration with audit, admission, encryption, and kubelet settings.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/index.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/index.mdx): Creating Clusters section landing page that directs users to platform-specific Kubernetes cluster provisioning guides. It highlights required preparation for infrastructure resources and the x86-64-v2 CPU baseline for ACP MicroOS images, then lists available Huawei DCS, Huawei Cloud Stack, and VMware vSphere workflows plus planned bare-metal support.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/create-cluster-in-global.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/create-cluster-in-global.mdx): Baseline workflow for creating a VMware vSphere workload cluster from the `global` cluster using CAPV direct-to-vCenter mode, one datacenter, one NIC per node, and static IP allocation through `VSphereMachineConfigPool`. It covers environment validation, namespace and vCenter Secret setup, `Cluster` and `VSphereCluster`, vSphere CPI delivery by `ClusterResourceSet`, control plane and worker machine config pools, `VSphereMachineTemplate`, `KubeadmControlPlane`, `MachineDeployment`, local image preloading for air-gapped bootstrap, verification, and troubleshooting controller, CPI, IP allocation, datastore, clone-mode, VIP, and thumbprint issues.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/extension-scenarios.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/extension-scenarios.mdx): Extension guide for an already working baseline VMware vSphere cluster deployment. It explains how to add a second NIC, enable multiple datacenters with `VSphereFailureDomain` and `VSphereDeploymentZone`, assign worker `failureDomain` values, append persistent disks including raw application-managed disks, scale worker replicas by expanding `VSphereMachineConfigPool` slots first, and verify node, machine, and vSphere provider resources after each change.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/index.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/index.mdx): Overview for creating a VMware vSphere workload cluster from the global cluster using standard CAPV deployment, where the platform connects to vCenter and provisions virtual machines. It defines the recommended reading order and the baseline topology: one datacenter, one NIC per node, three control plane nodes, one worker node, and static IP allocation through VSphereMachineConfigPool.
- [docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/parameter-checklist.mdx](docs/en/create-cluster/vmware-vsphere/parameter-checklist.mdx): Pre-deployment checklist for VMware vSphere cluster creation covering management-cluster prerequisites, vCenter credentials and thumbprint, VM template requirements, load balancer VIP planning, Kubernetes baseline values, CIDRs, Kube-OVN, CoreDNS, etcd, and encryption settings. It maps placeholders for VSphereMachineConfigPool slots, static IPs, compute sizing, system and data disks, vSphere CPI settings, and optional extensions such as multiple datacenters, failure domains, and a second NIC.

## how-to

- [docs/en/how-to/index.mdx](docs/en/how-to/index.mdx): Lightweight How To section index that renders the task-oriented overview for operational guides. It contains no standalone procedure beyond the section title and overview component.
- [docs/en/how-to/machine-health-check.mdx](docs/en/how-to/machine-health-check.mdx): Guide for configuring Cluster API MachineHealthCheck resources on a management cluster so unhealthy Machines are remediated after Ready condition timeouts. It provides YAML examples for MachineDeployment and control plane checks and warns about rolling-update disk replacement, etcd leader transfer failure when kubelet is down, and quorum loss risk from aggressive control plane remediation.
- [docs/en/how-to/node-configuration.mdx](docs/en/how-to/node-configuration.mdx): Operational guide for propagating Machine metadata to Kubernetes Nodes with Cluster API, including controller-manager flags for syncing selected labels and annotations plus MachineDeployment and KubeadmControlPlane template fields. It also explains how to apply taints through machine templates and verify labels, annotations, and taints on Machine and Node resources with kubectl JSONPath commands.

## infrastructure

- [docs/en/infrastructure/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/infrastructure/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): Preparation checklist for Huawei Cloud Stack infrastructure resources before writing Secret, HCSMachineConfigPool, HCSMachineTemplate, KubeadmControlPlane, or HCSCluster manifests. It covers HCS credentials, region and project IDs, image and flavor API values, disk layout, VPC and subnet inventory, multi-NIC limits, ELB fixed-address planning, static IP pools, rollout considerations, and value-to-YAML mappings.
- [docs/en/infrastructure/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/infrastructure/huawei-dcs.mdx): Detailed setup guide for Huawei DCS infrastructure resources using either the web UI or YAML, including cloud credentials, DCSIpHostnamePool IP pools, and DCSMachineTemplate machine templates. It documents DCS-specific prerequisites such as interface interconnection users, endpoint port 7443, MicroOS template ConfigMaps, required control plane and worker disks, datastore and shared-storage constraints, resource relationships, and links to cluster creation, node management, and upgrade workflows.
- [docs/en/infrastructure/index.mdx](docs/en/infrastructure/index.mdx): Infrastructure Resources section entry point for configuring prerequisites needed before cluster creation on supported IaaS platforms. It summarizes cloud credentials, IP pools, machine templates, network inventory, and load balancer planning, then links to Huawei DCS and Huawei Cloud Stack provider-specific instructions.

## install

- [docs/en/install/bare-metal.mdx](docs/en/install/bare-metal.mdx): Placeholder page for the planned Bare Metal Infrastructure Provider installation content. It states that the provider documentation is not yet available and points readers to the Bare Metal provider overview for background information.
- [docs/en/install/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/install/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): Installation workflow for the Huawei Cloud Stack Infrastructure Provider, requiring access to the global cluster and Customer Portal. It tells administrators to download the Alauda Container Platform Kubeadm Provider and HCS Infrastructure Provider plugins, upload and install them through ACP plugin workflows, then verify HCS pods and CRDs such as hcsclusters, hcsmachines, hcsmachinetemplates, and hcsmachineconfigpools.
- [docs/en/install/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/install/huawei-dcs.mdx): Installation workflow for the Huawei DCS Infrastructure Provider, including prerequisites for global cluster access and Customer Portal downloads. It directs administrators to install both the Alauda Container Platform Kubeadm Provider and DCS Infrastructure Provider, then verify DCS pods and CRDs such as dcsclusters, dcsmachines, dcsmachinetemplates, and dcsiphostnamepools.
- [docs/en/install/index.mdx](docs/en/install/index.mdx): Installation section overview for Immutable Infrastructure provider plugins and their shared requirement for the Alauda Container Platform Kubeadm Provider. It outlines the common workflow to download a provider plugin from Customer Portal, upload it to ACP, install it on the global cluster, and choose platform-specific guidance for Huawei DCS, Huawei Cloud Stack, VMware vSphere, or planned Bare Metal support.
- [docs/en/install/vmware-vsphere.mdx](docs/en/install/vmware-vsphere.mdx): Step-by-step installation page for the VMware vSphere Infrastructure Provider on the global cluster. It covers downloading the Kubeadm and VMware vSphere provider packages from Customer Portal, uploading and installing them as cluster plugins, and verifying module info and registered vSphere infrastructure CRDs with kubectl.

## machine-configuration

- [docs/en/machine-configuration/index.mdx](docs/en/machine-configuration/index.mdx): Machine Configuration section index that renders an overview for node configuration management content. The page is intentionally minimal and serves as the navigation entry for MachineConfig-related operational guidance.
- [docs/en/machine-configuration/managing.mdx](docs/en/machine-configuration/managing.mdx): Guide to managing node configuration with MachineConfig objects using Ignition spec 3.4, including writing files, overriding systemd units, setting SSH authorized keys for the boot user, and recovering from configuration drift. The examples show Chrony configuration via base64 file contents, disabling chronyd.service, applying worker-role labels, and forcing reapplication with /run/machine-config-daemon-force.
- [docs/en/machine-configuration/node_disruption.mdx](docs/en/machine-configuration/node_disruption.mdx): Explains how Machine Configuration node disruption policies control what happens when MachineConfig-managed files, systemd units, or SSH public keys change. It covers the default cluster MachineConfiguration behavior, available actions such as Reboot, None, Drain, Restart, and DaemonReload, and YAML examples for changing default file handling or applying actions to a specific file path.
- [docs/en/machine-configuration/overview.mdx](docs/en/machine-configuration/overview.mdx): Covers Machine Configuration concepts for Immutable Infrastructure, including MachineConfig, MachineConfigPool, rendered configs, worker custom pools, Ignition v3.4.0 format, update ordering, maxUnavailable, and configuration drift detection by machine-config-daemon. It also walks through the node update sequence and kubectl commands for checking pool, MachineConfig, and per-node desired/current configuration status.

## manage-nodes

- [docs/en/manage-nodes/bare-metal.mdx](docs/en/manage-nodes/bare-metal.mdx): Placeholder page for future bare metal worker node management guidance. The current content identifies the topic as planned and indicates detailed instructions will be added when bare metal node-management development begins.
- [docs/en/manage-nodes/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/manage-nodes/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): Provides the Huawei Cloud Stack worker node lifecycle workflow using Cluster API Machine resources, including HCSMachineConfigPool network entries, HCSMachineTemplate VM and volume settings, KubeadmConfigTemplate bootstrap data, and MachineDeployment orchestration. It explains HCS-specific subnet selection rules, required kubelet/containerd/cpaas data volumes, scaling, rolling replacement for infrastructure and Kubernetes upgrades, verification commands, and troubleshooting for provisioning or node join failures.
- [docs/en/manage-nodes/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/manage-nodes/huawei-dcs.mdx): Details Huawei DCS worker node management through Cluster API resources, from DCSIpHostnamePool allocation and DCSMachineTemplate VM, storage, network, and bootstrap configuration to MachineDeployment rollout control. It covers scaling up and down, targeted machine deletion with the cluster.x-k8s.io/delete-machine annotation, template and bootstrap updates, Kubernetes upgrade references, and web UI node pool management available with Fleet Essentials and DCS Infrastructure Provider 1.0.13 or later.
- [docs/en/manage-nodes/index.mdx](docs/en/manage-nodes/index.mdx): Landing page for Immutable Infrastructure worker node management that directs readers to platform-specific procedures for Huawei DCS, Huawei Cloud Stack, VMware vSphere, and planned bare metal support. It also points to related upgrade and Machine Configuration topics for broader node lifecycle work.
- [docs/en/manage-nodes/vmware-vsphere.mdx](docs/en/manage-nodes/vmware-vsphere.mdx): Explains VMware vSphere worker node management after cluster creation using VSphereMachineConfigPool, VSphereMachineTemplate, KubeadmConfigTemplate, and MachineDeployment resources. It covers scaling out by adding config pool slots, rolling VM specification and bootstrap template updates through immutable templates, rollback by restoring the previous template reference, status verification commands, and troubleshooting slot allocation, IP, DNS, VM template, and VSphereVM address issues.

## overview

- [docs/en/overview/architecture.mdx](docs/en/overview/architecture.mdx): Introduces the Immutable Infrastructure architecture as a Cluster API-based, Kubernetes-native platform for declarative cluster lifecycle management. It describes the roles of the kubeadm bootstrap provider, kubeadm control plane provider, and infrastructure providers such as DCS, including node initialization, certificate handling, highly available control planes, VM provisioning, networking, storage, upgrades, health monitoring, and GitOps workflows.
- [docs/en/overview/feature.mdx](docs/en/overview/feature.mdx): Summarizes Immutable Infrastructure capabilities for enterprise Kubernetes lifecycle management, including immutable OS cluster creation, automated compute provisioning, cluster deletion, horizontal scaling, Kubernetes upgrades, and provider-agnostic infrastructure management. It highlights current DCS and VMware vSphere support, compute resource options, and use cases for highly available control planes, workload-driven scaling, rolling zero-downtime upgrades, and multiple node pools with tailored resource profiles.
- [docs/en/overview/index.mdx](docs/en/overview/index.mdx): Minimal overview entry page that renders the shared Overview component for the Immutable Infrastructure documentation section. It serves as the top-level overview route rather than providing standalone technical guidance.
- [docs/en/overview/intro.mdx](docs/en/overview/intro.mdx): Introduces Immutable Infrastructure on the product platform as a declarative Cluster API approach to Kubernetes cluster management. It emphasizes infrastructure-as-code principles such as declarative configuration, version-controlled changes, reproducible deployments, and rollback to previous infrastructure states.
- [docs/en/overview/os-support-matrix.mdx](docs/en/overview/os-support-matrix.mdx): Lists ACP-provided MicroOS image compatibility for Immutable Infrastructure, including the x86_64 CPU requirement for the x86-64-v2 ISA baseline. It maps ACP releases to MicroOS image versions, Kubernetes versions, coredns, etcd, and pause image versions for node creation and upgrades.
- [docs/en/overview/providers/bare-metal.mdx](docs/en/overview/providers/bare-metal.mdx): Introduces the planned Bare Metal Infrastructure Provider for running Immutable Infrastructure directly on physical servers without virtualization. It explains key design considerations such as in-place upgrades for small clusters, data persistence, BMC-based remote management and self-healing, and differences from VM-based providers in upgrade, recovery, and disk handling.
- [docs/en/overview/providers/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/overview/providers/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): Introduces the Huawei Cloud Stack Infrastructure Provider for provisioning Kubernetes cluster infrastructure through Cluster API on HCS. It covers HCS integration with virtual machines, networks, storage, and load balancers, plus key features such as Kube-OVN support, ELB control plane load balancing, static IP configuration, HCSMachineConfigPool usage, security bootstrap options, availability zone selection, and related HCS resource types.
- [docs/en/overview/providers/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/overview/providers/huawei-dcs.mdx): Presents the Huawei DCS Infrastructure Provider for managing Kubernetes clusters on Huawei Datacenter Virtualization Solution with Cluster API. It covers VM, network, storage, high availability, and rolling update features; YAML and Fleet Essentials UI management modes; DCS cluster lifecycle and node pool capabilities; supported Kubernetes version linkage to the OS support matrix; core resources including DCSCluster, DCSMachine, DCSMachineTemplate, and DCSIpHostnamePool; and platform requirements.
- [docs/en/overview/providers/index.mdx](docs/en/overview/providers/index.mdx): Introduces Immutable Infrastructure's pluggable infrastructure providers and how they support cluster lifecycle management across Huawei DCS, Huawei Cloud Stack, VMware vSphere, and planned bare metal environments. It explains Fleet Essentials UI extension points for provider-specific cluster creation and management workflows, and summarizes the Cluster API architecture with infrastructure, kubeadm bootstrap, and kubeadm control plane providers.
- [docs/en/overview/providers/vmware-vsphere.mdx](docs/en/overview/providers/vmware-vsphere.mdx): Explains the VMware vSphere Infrastructure Provider for Immutable Infrastructure, built on Cluster API Provider vSphere and integrated with vCenter to provision and manage virtual machines, networks, and storage. It highlights node-slot allocation with `VSphereMachineConfigPool`, static IPs, multi-NIC layouts, failure domains, persistent disks, immutable-template rolling updates, and the main vSphere custom resources used by the documented workflows.
- [docs/en/overview/release_notes.mdx](docs/en/overview/release_notes.mdx): Summarizes release changes for Immutable Infrastructure, including the 2026-01-15 DCS Infrastructure Provider v1.0.9 and Kubeadm Provider v1.0.6 updates. It covers automatic encryption configuration after etcd key rotation, multi-NIC VM support, node labels, annotations, and taints through MachineDeployment and KubeadmControlPlane on supported ACP versions, plus initial cluster lifecycle, provider, and compute resource management capabilities.

## upgrade-cluster

- [docs/en/upgrade-cluster/bare-metal.mdx](docs/en/upgrade-cluster/bare-metal.mdx): States that bare-metal Kubernetes cluster upgrade documentation is planned and not yet available. It notes that bare-metal upgrades will differ from VM-based platforms by using an in-place upgrade approach so local disk data can persist without lengthy physical machine reinstallation.
- [docs/en/upgrade-cluster/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx](docs/en/upgrade-cluster/huawei-cloud-stack.mdx): Provides a Huawei Cloud Stack Kubernetes upgrade workflow for highly available clusters using Cluster API rolling replacement of control plane and worker nodes. It covers prerequisites, the unsupported single-control-plane upgrade case, HCS data-disk data loss risks, `HCSMachineTemplate` creation and cleanup, `KubeadmControlPlane` patching, Kubernetes version updates, static IP pool considerations with `maxSurge: 0`, and worker upgrades through MachineDeployment resources.
- [docs/en/upgrade-cluster/huawei-dcs.mdx](docs/en/upgrade-cluster/huawei-dcs.mdx): Covers Phase 2 Kubernetes upgrades for Huawei DCS clusters after the Distribution Version upgrade, with both YAML and Fleet Essentials web UI workflows. It details prerequisites such as Ready nodes, reachable control plane, IP Pool capacity, compatible VM templates, and explains control plane and worker upgrades through `DCSMachineTemplate`, `KubeadmControlPlane`, and `MachineDeployment`, including UI node pool sequencing, upgrade constraints, cross-minor upgrade ordering, and troubleshooting.
- [docs/en/upgrade-cluster/index.mdx](docs/en/upgrade-cluster/index.mdx): Defines the overall Immutable Infrastructure cluster upgrade model: first upgrade the ACP Distribution Version through Aligned Extensions, then upgrade Kubernetes versions on node pools. It explains the web UI flow for reviewing modifications, acknowledging plugin impact, starting the Aligned Extensions upgrade, post-upgrade verification, prerequisites such as healthy Ready nodes and IP capacity, and links to provider-specific Phase 2 guides for DCS, HCS, and vSphere.
- [docs/en/upgrade-cluster/vmware-vsphere.mdx](docs/en/upgrade-cluster/vmware-vsphere.mdx): Describes the VMware vSphere Kubernetes upgrade process after the distribution-version upgrade, centered on Cluster API resources and immutable `VSphereMachineTemplate` replacements. It walks through creating new control plane and worker templates, removing server-generated metadata, patching `KubeadmControlPlane` and `MachineDeployment` with target Kubernetes versions and template references, monitoring rollouts, rolling back failed upgrades, and verifying node readiness and vSphere CPI availability.
